preferences.languageHint: Choose the language for the app interface.
preferences.currencyHint: Switches the currency symbol and number formatting. Stored amounts are unchanged.
preferences.helpTooltipsHint: Show small ℹ icons next to labels with explanations. Hide them once you're familiar with the app.

split.intro: Set how shared expenses are divided. The percentages must add up to 100%.

recurring.empty: No recurring expenses set up yet

categories.name: Name
categories.rolloverMonthHint: Unused budget from last month carries over
categories.rolloverYearHint: Unused budget from all months this year carries over

sidebar.intro: Reorder pages, hide items, or switch to a flat layout.

insights.title: Insights
insights.debtAlertBody: Debt payments represent {{rate}}% of income this month

dashboard.netFlow: Income minus debt payments minus investment deposits. The actual cash left in your bank accounts for the period. Credit-card charges are tracked separately under Expenses.
dashboard.savingsRate: Net Flow as a percentage of Income. Uses the same cash-flow formula: (Income − Debt − Investments) ÷ Income.
dashboard.income: Paychecks plus transfers received in the period. Does not include investment gains.
dashboard.expenses: Credit-card charges and cash spending in the period. Informational only — does not reduce Net Flow directly. The real outflow is the Debt Paid when you pay the card.
dashboard.debtPaid: Money sent to credit cards in the period. This is the real cash leaving your bank.
dashboard.spendingTrends: Top 7 spending categories across the window: last 7 months when viewing monthly, 3 months for a quarter, 12 months for a year.
dashboard.snapshotNetWorth: Assets (investments) minus liabilities (mortgage balance) at the end of the selected period.
dashboard.snapshotMortgage: Most recent mortgage balance on or before the end of the selected period. Decreases as payments are recorded.
dashboard.snapshotDebtPaid: Total debt payments during the selected period. Same value as the Debt Paid KPI.
dashboard.insightsDebtAlert: Flags when debt payments exceed 36% of income — the common rule of thumb for a healthy debt load.
dashboard.debtRatioDonut: Debt payments ÷ Income for the selected period. Turns red above 36%.
dashboard.recurringPaidPill: Shown when an expense this month matches the recurring template's name (case-insensitive). Won't match if you edited the description.

cashFlow.income: Paychecks plus transfers received in the selected period. Does not include investment gains.
cashFlow.expenses: Credit-card and cash charges in the period. Informational — not subtracted from Total Savings. The real outflow happens when you pay the card (Debt).
cashFlow.totalSavings: Income − Debt − Investments. The Cash Flow Model: subtracts actual bank outflows (debt payments + investment deposits), not credit-card charges.
cashFlow.savingsRate: Total Savings ÷ Income × 100. Same formula as the Dashboard's Savings Rate.
cashFlow.sankeyOverview: Paycheck and Transfers flow in from the left into Income. On the right, Income splits into Savings (cash left), Debt (card payments leaving your bank), and Investments (deposits).
cashFlow.netColumn: Income − Debt for the row's period. Cash Flow Model — does not subtract expenses (credit-card charges are tracked separately).
cashFlow.variance: Period B − Period A. Positive means the metric went up. Color encodes whether that's favorable (green) or unfavorable (red) for each metric.
cashFlow.quickInsight: Auto-generated sentence highlighting the metric that moved the most between Period A and Period B.

reports.totalIncome: Paychecks plus transfers received in the selected period. Does not include investment gains.
reports.expenses: Credit-card and cash charges in the period. Informational — not subtracted from Net Cash Flow. The real outflow happens when you pay the card (Debt).
reports.netCashFlow: Income − Debt − Investments. Cash Flow Model: subtracts actual bank outflows, not credit-card charges.
reports.savingsRate: Net Cash Flow ÷ Income × 100. Same formula as the Cash Flow page and the Dashboard.
reports.sankeyOverview: Paycheck and Transfers flow in from the left into Income. On the right, Income splits into Savings (cash left), Debt (card payments leaving your bank), and Investments (deposits).
reports.netColumn: Income − Debt for the row's period. Cash Flow Model — does not subtract expenses (credit-card charges are tracked separately).
reports.spendingByCategory: Donut breakdown of expenses for the selected period. The center value is the total of all categories shown.
reports.incomeVsOutflow: "Outflow" here means debt payments only — the real cash leaving your bank. Credit-card charges (Expenses) are tracked separately and aren't included.

budget.totalBudget: Sum of all category budgets, scaled to the selected period (monthly × 3 for quarterly, × 12 for yearly).
budget.remaining: Total Budget − Total Spent. Stops at zero — if you're over budget the value here is zero and the Over Budget count rises.
budget.incomeSection: Budgets for paychecks and transfers. Click any value to set a manual monthly target; if you leave it empty, the app uses your 6-month average instead.
budget.incomeReceived: Actual paycheck income and transfers received in the selected period.
budget.incomeRemaining: Income uses inverted polarity: red when you have NOT yet received the targeted amount, green when you over-received. Opposite of how expense Remaining works.
budget.expensesSection: Category budgets vs actual spending. Categories with a ↻ icon have rollover enabled — unused budget from a prior month or YTD carries forward and increases the effective budget for the current period.
budget.expensesBudget: Category budgets are stored monthly and scaled to the selected period. Quarterly view shows monthly × 3; yearly shows monthly × 12.
budget.expensesRemaining: Budget − Spent. Green when under budget, red when over.

debt.paidThisPeriod: Sum of debt payments recorded in the selected period. This is real cash leaving your bank — same value used in Dashboard's Debt Paid KPI.
debt.remainingDebt: All-time total expenses minus all-time payments (not filtered by period). A rough running balance of how much you still owe across all cards.
debt.debtRatio: Paid This Period ÷ Income for the same period × 100. The Dashboard flags this as a Debt Alert when it climbs above 36%.
debt.topCard: The card that received the largest payment total in the selected period.
debt.cardBalances: Running balance per card: all-time charges on the card minus all-time payments. Updates as you add expenses and record payments.

portfolio.totalValue: Sum of every portfolio's current balance. All-time, not filtered by period — this is your net investment position right now.
portfolio.totalYield: Sum of GAIN movements within the selected period. The actual returns you earned (interest, dividends, appreciation marked as gain).
portfolio.totalDeposits: Sum of DEPOSIT movements within the selected period. New money you added to your investment accounts.
portfolio.holdings: Each portfolio with its current balance and Return %. Return % = cumulative gains ÷ (cumulative deposits + transfers in). Withdrawals don't reduce the basis.
portfolio.movementTypes: DEPOSIT = new money in. GAIN = returns earned (interest, appreciation). WITHDRAWAL = money out. TRANSFER = balance moved between portfolios (no net change to Total Value).

mortgage.projectedPayoff: Estimated payoff date computed by amortizing the current balance at the contractual Principal + Interest payment. Does NOT assume any future extra payments.
mortgage.timeSaved: Months you've shaved off the original amortization schedule by paying extra principal. Compares your actual trajectory against the contractual schedule.
mortgage.interestSaved: Cumulative interest avoided thanks to extra principal payments — money that would have accrued on the original schedule but no longer does.
mortgage.extraCapital: Sum of every Extra Capital amount you've recorded on payments. This is principal-only — it pays the loan down faster without touching the contractual P+I.
mortgage.minPayment: The minimum payment value stored in your config. Note: projections (Projected Payoff, Time Saved, Interest Saved) use the contractual Principal + Interest from your loan terms, which may differ slightly from this stored value.
mortgage.balanceTrajectory: Original Schedule = balance trajectory if you only paid the contractual P+I. Actual Balance = your real trajectory including any extra principal payments. The gap between them is what your extras are doing.
mortgage.simulator: What-if tool: enter a one-time extra principal deposit to see how it would shorten the loan, change the payoff date, and reduce total interest paid.

netWorth.netWorth: Total Assets − Total Liabilities. Snapshot of your financial position right now (not period-filtered).
netWorth.totalAssets: Sum of every portfolio's current balance. The app currently only tracks investment portfolios as assets — bank cash is not modeled.
netWorth.totalLiabilities: Currently just your current mortgage balance. Credit-card debt is tracked separately on the Debt page and is not included here.
netWorth.paidOff: Share of the original mortgage principal you've paid down. (Original Principal − Current Balance) ÷ Original Principal.
netWorth.allocation: Each portfolio's share of Total Assets. Helps you see whether one position is dominating your net worth.

sharedBalance.totalShared: Sum of every shared expense recorded since the most recent zero-balance settlement (or all-time if no settlement is on file). Personal expenses are excluded.
sharedBalance.splitRatio: The default split percentage from Settings → Shared Expenses Split. Individual expenses can override this with their own ratio at entry time, which then takes precedence over the default.
sharedBalance.netSettlement: The current balance owed between users for shared expenses. Starts fresh from the last time the balance was zero (not the last payment) — partial payments keep accumulated expenses visible until you fully settle.
sharedBalance.sharedSinceLast: Every shared expense and cash entry since the most recent zero-balance settlement. Partial payments do NOT reset this list — only a settlement that brings the balance to zero does.

income.grossIncome: Sum of paycheck gross amounts in the period (before taxes withheld). Falls back to the net (MXN) amount when a paycheck doesn't record a gross value.
income.netIncome: Sum of paycheck net amounts minus any manual tax entries in the same period. This is the actual take-home you saw hit your account.
income.transfers: All transfers received in the period (household contributions, rental income, gifts, etc.) — every income source that isn't a paycheck.
income.rateChart: USD/MXN exchange rate tracked over time. Auto-calculated whenever you enter a paycheck with both USD and MXN amounts.
income.manualTaxes: One-off tax entries outside of paycheck withholding (quarterly estimated tax, year-end balances, etc.). Subtracted from gross to compute Net Income.

expenses.total: Sum of every credit-card and cash charge in the period. Informational only — for what actually leaves your bank look at Debt Paid (when you pay the card).
expenses.typeColumn: Shared = expense split between users using the Split Ratio (from Settings, or a per-expense override). Personal = belongs only to whoever paid it.
expenses.categoryBudgets: Quick view of category budgets vs actual spending in the selected period. For rollover settings and manual income targets, edit on the Budget page.

transactions.totalEntries: Count of every transaction in the period: paychecks, transfers received, expenses, and debt payments combined.
transactions.typeColumn: Paycheck = salary received. Transfer = non-salary income (household, rental, etc.). Expense = a charge you made. Debt = a payment you sent to a credit card.

forms.expenseSubCategory: Free-text sub-grouping under the main category (e.g. "Whole Foods" under Groceries). Suggestions come from sub-categories you've used before on the same category.
forms.expensePaidBy: Which user paid for this expense upfront. Affects the shared-balance ledger when the expense is marked Shared.
forms.expenseShared: When checked, the expense is split between users using the default Split Ratio from Settings (which can be overridden per-expense via the Shared Balance flow). Unchecked = personal expense, only the payer is on the hook.
forms.investmentType: DEPOSIT adds new money. GAIN records earned returns. WITHDRAWAL removes money. TRANSFER moves balance to another portfolio (asks for a destination — no net change to Total Value).
forms.mortgageExtraCapital: Principal-only payment on top of the contractual Principal + Interest. Reduces the loan balance faster, shortens the payoff date, and lowers total interest paid. Auto-computed when your total payment exceeds the contractual P+I.
forms.settlementAmount: Pre-filled with the current net balance so a full payment zeroes out the ledger and starts a fresh "Since Last Settlement" window. Enter a smaller value for a partial payment (the ledger won't reset).

popover.footer: Monthly · Enter to save · Esc to cancel

chart.empty6mo: No data for the last 6 months

empty.noSharedSinceLast: No shared expenses since last settlement

options.followingDesc: Only new expenses will use the new split. Existing transactions keep their current ratio.
options.currentDesc: Apply to all shared expenses since the last zero balance, plus new ones.
options.allTimeDesc: Retroactively apply to every shared expense ever recorded.

snapshot.invalidBody: This link may be corrupted or incomplete.

chart.title: Balance Over Time
chart.empty: Add payments to see balance progression

simulator.enterAmount: Enter an amount to see the impact

empty.noPortfoliosCta: No portfolios yet. Add one to get started.

empty.noPayments: No debt payments in this period

empty.noTaxesPeriod: No tax records in this period

loading.hint: This may take a few seconds per image

error.noTransactions: No transactions found. Try the other bank.

review.duplicateBanner: Possible duplicate — same date & amount already exists
review.creditsExcludedOne: {{count}} payment/credit excluded (track those on Debt Payments)
review.creditsExcludedOther: {{count}} payments/credits excluded (track those on Debt Payments)

done.addedBody: They are now visible in your expense list.
